chapters.title: Greetings & Introductions
chapters.description: Learn essential Hindi greetings and how to introduce yourself.

lessons.explanation: 'Namaste' is the universal Hindi greeting. It literally means 'I bow to the divine in you'. Used for both hello and goodbye.

lessons.prompt: मेरा नाम ___ है। (My name is Rahul.)
lessons.explanation: 'Mera naam [name] hai' = My name is [name]. 'Mera' = my, 'naam' = name, 'hai' = is.

lessons.prompt: What is the correct response to 'Aap kaise hain?'
lessons.explanation: 'Main theek hoon, dhanyavaad' = I am fine, thank you. 'Theek' = fine/okay, 'dhanyavaad' = thank you.

lessons.prompt: मैं भारत से ___। (I am from India.)
lessons.explanation: 'Hoon' is the first-person singular form of 'hona' (to be). 'Main Bharat se hoon' = I am from India.

learningMaterial.title: Greetings & Introductions
learningMaterial.intro: Learn essential Hindi greetings and how to introduce yourself.

concepts.explanation: 'Namaste' is the universal Hindi greeting. It literally means 'I bow to the divine in you'. Used for both hello and goodbye.
'Mera naam [name] hai' = My name is [name]. 'Mera' = my, 'naam' = name, 'hai' = is.
'Aap' = you (formal/respectful). 'Kaise' = how. 'Hain' = are (plural/respectful form).
'Main theek hoon, dhanyavaad' = I am fine, thank you. 'Theek' = fine/okay, 'dhanyavaad' = thank you.
'Hoon' is the first-person singular form of 'hona' (to be). 'Main Bharat se hoon' = I am from India.

chapters.title: Hindi Script — Devanagari Basics
chapters.description: Learn to recognize and read the Devanagari script used for Hindi.

lessons.explanation: 'अ' = short 'a' sound (like 'u' in 'but'). 'आ' = long 'aa'. Hindi has 11 vowels in Devanagari.
Devanagari uses vowel marks (matras) attached to consonants. क (ka) + ा (aa matra) = का (kaa).

lessons.prompt: What word does this spell? न + म + स + ् + त + े
lessons.hint: न=na, म=ma, स=sa (with halant making it 's'), त=ta, े=e matra. What familiar word is this?

lessons.explanation: भारत = Bharat (India). भ = bha, ा = aa, र = ra, त = ta. The official Hindi name for India.
'Paani' (पानी) = water. One of the most essential vocabulary words. प=pa, ा=aa, न=na, ी=ii.

learningMaterial.title: Hindi Script — Devanagari Basics
learningMaterial.intro: Learn to recognize and read the Devanagari script used for Hindi.

concepts.explanation: 'अ' = short 'a' sound (like 'u' in 'but'). 'आ' = long 'aa'. Hindi has 11 vowels in Devanagari.
Devanagari uses vowel marks (matras) attached to consonants. क (ka) + ा (aa matra) = का (kaa).
न=na, म=ma, स=sa (with halant making it 's'), त=ta, े=e matra. What familiar word is this?
भारत = Bharat (India). भ = bha, ा = aa, र = ra, त = ta. The official Hindi name for India.
'Paani' (पानी) = water. One of the most essential vocabulary words. प=pa, ा=aa, न=na, ी=ii.

chapters.title: Numbers & Basic Math
chapters.description: Count in Hindi and use numbers in everyday situations.

lessons.prompt: मुझे ___ किलो चावल चाहिए। (I need two kilos of rice.)
lessons.explanation: 'Do' (दो) = two. 'Mujhe ... chahiye' = I need/want. Essential shopping phrase.

lessons.hint: 'Iski keemat' = its price. 'Kitni hai' = how much is. Also: 'Yeh kitne ka hai?' (How much is this?)

lessons.prompt: मेरे पास ___ रुपए हैं। (I have a hundred rupees.)
lessons.explanation: 'Sau' (सौ) = hundred. 'Mere paas ... rupaye hain' = I have ... rupees. 'Mere paas' = I have (lit: near me).

learningMaterial.title: Numbers & Basic Math
learningMaterial.intro: Count in Hindi and use numbers in everyday situations.

concepts.explanation: 'Do' (दो) = two. 'Mujhe ... chahiye' = I need/want. Essential shopping phrase.
'Iski keemat' = its price. 'Kitni hai' = how much is. Also: 'Yeh kitne ka hai?' (How much is this?)
'Sau' (सौ) = hundred. 'Mere paas ... rupaye hain' = I have ... rupees. 'Mere paas' = I have (lit: near me).

chapters.title: Basic Sentence Structure
chapters.description: Build Hindi sentences using subject-object-verb word order.

lessons.prompt: What is the basic word order in Hindi?
lessons.explanation: Hindi is SOV: 'Main chai peeta hoon' = I tea drink (I drink tea). The verb comes at the end.
'Bolta hoon' = I speak (masculine). Feminine: 'bolti hoon'. Hindi verbs agree with gender.

lessons.hint: 'Voh' = she/he. 'Sabzi' = vegetables. 'Khaati hai' = eats (feminine form). 'Hai' = is/are.

lessons.prompt: What is the negative particle in Hindi?
lessons.explanation: 'Nahin' = no/not. Place before the verb: 'Main nahin jaata' = I don't go. 'Nahin' can also stand alone as 'no'.

lessons.prompt: क्या आप चाय ___? (Do you drink tea?)
lessons.explanation: 'Kya' at sentence start makes a yes/no question. 'Peete hain' = drink (masculine plural/formal). 'Kya aap chai peete hain?' = Do you drink tea?

learningMaterial.title: Basic Sentence Structure
learningMaterial.intro: Build Hindi sentences using subject-object-verb word order.

concepts.explanation: Hindi is SOV: 'Main chai peeta hoon' = I tea drink (I drink tea). The verb comes at the end.
'Bolta hoon' = I speak (masculine). Feminine: 'bolti hoon'. Hindi verbs agree with gender.
'Voh' = she/he. 'Sabzi' = vegetables. 'Khaati hai' = eats (feminine form). 'Hai' = is/are.
'Nahin' = no/not. Place before the verb: 'Main nahin jaata' = I don't go. 'Nahin' can also stand alone as 'no'.
'Kya' at sentence start makes a yes/no question. 'Peete hain' = drink (masculine plural/formal). 'Kya aap chai peete hain?' = Do you drink tea?

chapters.title: Food & Daily Life
chapters.description: Talk about food, meals, and everyday activities in Hindi.

lessons.prompt: What does 'मुझे भूख लगी है (mujhe bhookh lagi hai)' mean?
lessons.explanation: 'Bhookh lagna' = to feel hunger. 'Mujhe pyaas lagi hai' = I am thirsty. Hunger and thirst are expressed differently in Hindi.

lessons.prompt: मैं सुबह ___ खाता हूँ। (I eat breakfast in the morning.)
lessons.explanation: 'Naashta' = breakfast. 'Dopahar ka khaana' = lunch. 'Raat ka khaana' = dinner.
'Kripaya' = please (formal). Less formal: just add the polite tone. 'Dhanyavaad' = thank you. 'Maafi' = sorry.
'Chahiye' = need/want (essential word). 'Mujhe X chahiye' = I need/want X. Very useful in daily life.

learningMaterial.title: Food & Daily Life
learningMaterial.intro: Talk about food, meals, and everyday activities in Hindi.

concepts.explanation: 'Bhookh lagna' = to feel hunger. 'Mujhe pyaas lagi hai' = I am thirsty. Hunger and thirst are expressed differently in Hindi.
'Naashta' = breakfast. 'Dopahar ka khaana' = lunch. 'Raat ka khaana' = dinner.
'Yeh khaana' = this food. 'Bahut' = very. 'Swaadisht' = delicious/tasty.
'Kripaya' = please (formal). Less formal: just add the polite tone. 'Dhanyavaad' = thank you. 'Maafi' = sorry.
'Chahiye' = need/want (essential word). 'Mujhe X chahiye' = I need/want X. Very useful in daily life.
